title: Bridge Essay: Impaired corollary discharge as a predictor of reality monitoring deficits in schizophrenia: Implications for auditory verbal hallucinations.
concept: Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.

body.text: This bridge paper connects **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.

**Impaired corollary discharge as a predictor of reality monitoring deficits in schizophrenia: Implications for auditory verbal hallucinations.** — Schizophr Res (2025). DOI: 10.1016/j.schres.2025.07.003. PMID: 40633307. BACKGROUND: Auditory verbal hallucinations (AVH) in schizophrenia are linked to reality monitoring (RM) impairment in source monitoring tasks. However, little is known about its neurobiological basis. Corollary discharge (CD), a predictive neural mechanism distinguishing self-generated from external stimuli, is hypothesized to underlie these impairments, contributing to the externalization bias of internal thoughts (EB) and the genesis of AVH.
